transliteration: avyakto 'yam acintyo 'yam avikƒБryo 'yam ucyate | tasmƒБd evaбєГ viditvainaбєГ nƒБnu≈Ыocitum arhasi ||
literal_meaning: This (soul) is said to be unmanifest, inconceivable, unchangeable; therefore, knowing it thus, you ought not to grieve.
contextual_meaning: Krishna reframes ArjunaвАЩs grief by pointing to the eternal, uninjurable dimension of identityвАФthe soulвАФrendering bodily loss analogous to discarding worn-out garments.
psychological_state: Cognitive shift from egoic narrative ('I will kill') to trans-personal witness ('no weapon can harm the soul')вАФan early mindfulness-based decentering technique.
trigger_scenario: A therapist telling a bereaved client: 'The love you shared is not in the body thatвАЩs gone; itвАЩs an invisible, irreducible presence you can still converse with.'
anchor_text: The real you never dies, so grieve only for the body, not the soul.
